description: WebFlash-compatible firmware build matrix and the sole release-eligibility source of truth. Each entry is validated by tests/validate_webflash_builds.py against config/webflash-compatibility.json. Concrete preview / advanced-preview release targets for every buildable product (including the fan-driver and TRIAC targets that are NOT WebFlash-importable) are enumerated in config/preview-release-targets.json (RELEASE-PREVIEW-TARGETS-ALL-PRODUCTS-001); a row is added here only with recorded build proof. RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001 added the three preview rows for the compile-validated room-bundle preview targets (Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED), each addressed via its products/webflash wrapper, on the preview (non-stable) channel with hosted-compile evidence (run 26821900127); those rows are release-eligibility METADATA only (release_state: metadata-ready-unpublished) — no firmware binary, GitHub Release, tag, manifest.json, or firmware/sources.json is produced, and the consuming candidate bundles stay hidden / not buyable. The stable Release-One row (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ) and the published VentIQ LED preview row are unchanged. CI-PIPELINE-CLARITY-001 P4a de-listed the Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED row (it was never built or served — no upstream binary, not in the served set): its build row was removed here and its config/product-catalog.json entry demoted from preview to hardware-pending (webflash_build_matrix false), so the release matrix no longer advertises a product that has no artifact; the catalog entry is preserved so it can be built properly later. The distinct published VentIQ LED preview (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ-LED) is unaffected. HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md, owner decision of record, 2026-07-09) retired the bench-proof documentation requirement as a release gate and, per owner declaration that all boards are bench-working, added nine release-eligibility-metadata rows (release_state metadata-ready-unpublished; no binary / Release / tag / manifest cut): Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED (re-listed, reversing the CI-PIPELINE-CLARITY-001 P4a de-list), the six FanPWM / FanDAC configs on the preview channel, and the two FanRelay room bundles on the experimental channel only (mains-adjacent lane per COMPLIANCE-001; never stable). The former fan-token guardrail ('no fan row in this file') was revised by the owner to channel teeth: fan configs are NEVER on the stable channel.

builds.stable_blocker: LED preview-to-stable gauntlet (operator flash proof, bench verification, stable promotion).

builds.release_note_warning: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
builds.stable_blocker: Released under owner risk-acceptance HW-AIRIQ-WAIVER-2026-06. AirIQ sensor-stack bench accuracy (SPS30, SGP41, SCD41, BMP390) not verified. Owner risk-acceptance, not hardware verification. S360-410 PoE PSU covered by HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06.
builds.notes: Kitchen PoE AirIQ + RoomIQ build row, added on the preview channel by RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001 and kept on the preview channel at version 1.0.9 because the owner waiver is not hardware verification and must not cut a stable build. Stable promotion remains blocked by HW-AIRIQ-WAIVER-2026-06 (owner risk-acceptance, NOT hardware verification): AirIQ sensor-stack bench accuracy (SPS30, SGP41, SCD41, BMP390) is not verified, and the S360-410 PoE PSU stays covered by HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06. The S360-KIT-KITCHEN-P bundle stays hidden / not buyable; NOT recommended, NOT a customer default, NOT in release_one_required_configs; commercial_posture buyable / recommended / customer_default / visibility stay owner-decided and were not flipped here. No hardware / bench / compliance proof is claimed.

builds.stable_blocker: Resolved by owner waiver HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06: S360-410 PoE PSU released under owner risk-acceptance (NOT hardware verification); schematic_status stays cataloged_unverified and the remaining PoE bench / isolation evidence was waived, not measured. Prior blocker was S360-410 PoE-PSU schematic verification (PRODUCT-POE-410-001 / PACKAGE-POE-410-001).
builds.notes: Bedroom PoE RoomIQ-only build row (the smallest SELV RoomIQ-only PoE bundle), added on the preview channel by RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001 and now promoted to the stable channel at version 1.0.5 so Create Release can cut a stable build. This build-matrix change cuts NO build: no firmware binary, GitHub Release, tag, manifest.json, or firmware/sources.json is produced here. Stable promotion is authorised by owner waiver HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06 (owner risk-acceptance, NOT hardware verification): S360-410 schematic_status stays cataloged_unverified and the remaining PoE bench / isolation evidence was waived, not measured. RoomIQ on-device flash verification is not yet done. The S360-KIT-BEDROOM-P bundle stays hidden / not buyable; NOT recommended, NOT a customer default, NOT in release_one_required_configs; commercial_posture buyable / recommended / customer_default / visibility stay owner-decided and were not flipped here. No hardware / bench / compliance proof is claimed.

builds.release_note_warning: EXPERIMENTAL — SELF-BUILD MAINS HARDWARE. This firmware targets an open-source mains-voltage board design that Sense360 does not sell, supply, or place on the market in any form. If you build this board you are the manufacturer of your own device and act entirely at your own risk. Functional bench completion is NOT a safety, EMC, or compliance certification. Mains wiring must only be performed by a competent person. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death.
builds.stable_blocker: Stable / full release stays gated: a stable customer release would be a placing-on-the-market act, which reopens COMPLIANCE-001 and requires an external safety + EMC assessment BEFORE that act (docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md section 4). Never promoted to stable by this row.
hardware_requirements: Sense360 TRIAC module (S360-320, self-build open-source CERN-OHL-P board; never sold or supplied by Sense360)
features: Mains TRIAC phase-cut fan/lamp dimming on the S360-320 Sense360 TRIAC (experimental self-build; competent-person manual install only)
builds.notes: TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 experimental self-build mains build row for the S360-320 Sense360 TRIAC, published on the experimental channel (build channel 'experimental'; artifact suffix -experimental) via the experimental lane defined by COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 (config/release-channel-policy.json experimental_lane). It compiles the canonical product YAML products/sense360-ceiling-poe-ventiq-fantriac-roomiq.yaml via the products/webflash wrapper (no YAML functional change). The PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 half of the former blocker is cleared (operator-attested bench proof docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md); COMPLIANCE-001 is closed by market posture. Release-eligibility METADATA only (release_state metadata-ready-unpublished): this PR adds the row but does NOT cut a release tag, build a .bin, or write a manifest. NEVER stable, NEVER recommended, NEVER a customer default, NEVER buyable, NEVER in any kit or kit picker, NEVER in release_one_required_configs. The S360-320 is an open-source CERN-OHL-P design Sense360 never places on the market; builders self-source and self-build at their own risk. Functional bench completion is firmware-build + bench proof only and is NOT an electrical-safety, EMC, or compliance certification. Downstream WebFlash one-click import stays gated by WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001 (webflash_one_click_import_eligible false), unblocked only once the experimental release is cut. TRIAC blocker/status changes are human-review only, never auto-merged.

builds.stable_blocker: RELEASE-DAC-001 + FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001 (pending bench). Fan configs never ship on the stable channel.
hardware_requirements: Sense360 DAC module (S360-312, 0-10V analog fan driver, SELV)
features: 0-10V analog fan drive on the S360-312 twin GP8403 DACs (preview)
builds.notes: Promoted by owner declaration HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md): the bench-proof documentation requirement is retired as a release gate and hardware readiness is declared by the owner directly. Release-eligibility METADATA only (release_state metadata-ready-unpublished): this row cuts NO release tag, builds NO .bin, and writes NO manifest.json or firmware/sources.json here. Firmware-build proof only — no hardware / bench / compliance / safety / commercial-availability proof is claimed. NOT recommended, NOT a customer default, NOT buyable, NOT in any kit or kit picker, NOT in release_one_required_configs; kit / customer visibility is unchanged. REQUIRED FanDAC I2C address switch setting: the second GP8403 (IC2) is relocated from its 0x59 package default to 0x5A (fan_dac_2_i2c_address override in the bundle) so it cannot collide with the air-quality module SGP41 at 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus; the installer must set the IC2 DIP switch (SW2) to 0x5A to match. FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001 stays PENDING (docs/hardware/fandac-i2c-address-verification.md). This config is the documented address-overridden EXCEPTION to the fandac_conflicts_with_airiq one-click grammar mutex (config/webflash-compatibility.json address_overridden_exception_config_strings): the mutex stays TRUE for WebFlash one-click easy-mode, which cannot encode the DIP-switch override; this row is acknowledgement-gated advanced/manual preview only.

builds.release_note_warning: EXPERIMENTAL — MAINS-ADJACENT FAN RELAY. The S360-310 Sense360 Relay switches fan loads that may be mains-connected. Where mains AC switching is in scope, installation must only be performed by a competent person. This firmware is released under owner declaration HW-RELEASE-001 and carries NO electrical-safety, EMC, or compliance certification. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death.
builds.stable_blocker: NEVER stable from this lane: FanRelay is mains-adjacent contact switching, promoted to the experimental channel only per the COMPLIANCE-001 lane posture (docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md); a stable customer release stays gated by RELEASE-RELAY-001 (mains-safety / installation / competent-person evidence) and is NOT unblocked by HW-RELEASE-001.
hardware_requirements: Sense360 Relay module (S360-310, mains-adjacent contact switching)
features: On/off extract-fan relay switching on the S360-310 (experimental; where mains AC switching is in scope, competent-person installation only)
